title: EY sacks graduate employee after he allegedly accessed Australian PM’s bank account
summary_full: Two men – including one who worked for EY – appear in court after being charged over accessing restricted dataGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAn employee at one of Australia’s big four accounting firms has been sacked after he and another man allegedly accessed the prime minister Anthony Albanese’s personal banking account.The men, aged 21 and 25, faced court on Tuesday over the breach, which Australian federal police alleged occurred when the EY graduate was on secondment at the Commonwealth Bank. Continue reading...
